required_inclusions: State the business outcome the specialist owns and the decision surface it can influence without drifting into adjacent lanes.
Define specialty boundaries in terms of owned workflows, owned artifacts, and explicit refusal or handoff conditions.
Name the minimum tenant facts, systems of record, and retrieval dependencies required before the specialist can act safely.
Specify the evidence artifacts, output shapes, and traceability expectations the specialist must produce.
Document regulated, contractual, financial, privacy, or safety caveats that limit autonomous action.
List adjacent specialists and the conditions that require orchestrator return instead of improvised cross-functional ownership.
required_scenarios: Normal in-scope execution using realistic industry terminology and artifact shapes.
Boundary rejection when the request should move to an adjacent specialist.
Low-confidence handling when required facts, records, or authority sources are missing.
Conflicting-source or conflicting-policy handling that requires escalation instead of confident synthesis.
Tenant-specific adaptation handling that preserves the public baseline and records assumptions.
required_source_rules: Use primary or clearly authoritative sources before secondary explainers.
Explain why each source is authoritative for this specialty, not just generally relevant.
Record refresh interval, decay policy, and last review date for every source.
Prefer source mixes that cover policy, workflow, evidence, and terminology rather than only one of those dimensions.

required_inclusions: Define intake prerequisites, qualification checks, and what facts must be present before work can start.
Specify identity, eligibility, authorization, referral, reservation, or access checks when the lane depends on them.
Document queue states from intake through acceptance, rejection, or routing.
Name the exact triggers for refusal, escalation, or transfer to another lane before execution begins.
required_workflows: Escalation when required records or approvals are missing.
required_sources: Public guidance describing intake, eligibility, admission, authorization, or booking rules relevant to the lane.
required_scenarios: Missing prerequisite information before intake can be accepted.
Invalid, incomplete, or ineligible request.
Case that must be routed elsewhere before execution begins.
boundary_rules: Do not skip prerequisite validation just to move work forward.
Do not convert intake authority into substantive downstream approval authority.

required_inclusions: Define readiness criteria, required setup artifacts, and sequencing rules for bringing a user, account, member, or partner into service.
Specify permissions, access dependencies, education requirements, and readiness checks that gate go-live.
Document what must be confirmed before handoff into steady-state execution.
required_workflows: Readiness confirmation and handoff to steady-state operations.
required_sources: Public onboarding, enrollment, credentialing, or training workflow guidance relevant to the lane.
required_scenarios: Setup blocked by missing prerequisites.
Access or permission mismatch during onboarding.
Request to mark onboarding complete without readiness evidence.
boundary_rules: Do not treat onboarding completion as proof that downstream operational or compliance obligations are satisfied.

required_inclusions: Define the planning horizon, constraints, allocation rules, and downstream impact of schedule or priority changes.
Specify what inputs are authoritative for capacity, timing, and resource decisions.
Document which decisions are recommendations versus final approvals.
required_workflows: Demand or work intake for planning.
Schedule, priority, or allocation decision support.
required_sources: Public planning, scheduling, dispatch, or resource-allocation guidance relevant to the lane.
required_scenarios: Demand exceeds available capacity.
Conflicting priorities with no approved decision rule.
Request for final approval outside lane authority.
boundary_rules: Do not present a planning recommendation as a final budget, staffing, or operational approval.

required_inclusions: Define the asset or equipment states, maintenance triggers, uptime expectations, and evidence required to move work into or out of maintenance.
Specify preventive versus corrective workflow boundaries, planning assumptions, and approval gates.
Document when safety, engineering, warranty, or operations authority must approve the next step.
required_workflows: Escalation for safety, engineering, or outage-risk decisions.
required_sources: Public maintenance, reliability, asset-lifecycle, or field-service guidance relevant to the lane.
required_scenarios: Maintenance delayed by missing parts, approvals, or safety checks.
Conflicting uptime and maintenance priorities.
Request to defer or close maintenance without required evidence.
boundary_rules: Do not imply engineer-of-record, safety signoff, or warranty authority without explicit scope.

required_inclusions: Define the parties, dependencies, and prerequisite confirmations needed for safe handoff between teams or workflow stages.
Specify communication obligations, routing states, and blocker management rules.
Document what counts as successful transfer or closure.
required_sources: Public coordination, dispatch, handoff, or routing workflow guidance relevant to the lane.
required_scenarios: Missing prerequisite before a handoff can occur.
Conflicting schedules or dependency owners.
Attempt to force coordination into substantive approval authority.
boundary_rules: Do not turn coordination responsibility into ownership of the underlying specialist decision.

required_inclusions: Define the core in-scope lifecycle states, execution steps, and record-of-truth systems used to move work from accepted intake to completion.
Specify throughput expectations, exception queues, and completion evidence.
Document what execution can change directly versus what requires external approval.
required_sources: Public operating procedures, lifecycle-state definitions, or fulfillment workflow references relevant to the lane.
required_scenarios: Normal in-scope execution from accepted work to completion.
Processing blocked by missing upstream data or approvals.
Request that requires stepping outside documented execution authority.
boundary_rules: Do not absorb policy, legal, engineering, or executive authority just because the work sits in an execution queue.

required_inclusions: Define eligible service or support request types, service-level expectations, and what counts as resolution.
Specify communication expectations, evidence capture, and exception-handling rules.
Document when support or service work must escalate rather than improvising a workaround.
required_workflows: Escalation for policy, authority, or repeat-failure cases.
required_sources: Public service, support, client-delivery, or customer-workflow guidance relevant to the lane.
required_scenarios: Ineligible or unsupported request.
Repeat failure requiring escalation.
Request for unsupported workaround or discretionary exception.
boundary_rules: Do not override policy, pricing, or eligibility limits just to resolve a case.

required_inclusions: Define the supply, inventory, vendor, or procurement states the lane owns and the evidence required for changes.
Specify replenishment, sourcing, availability, and exception-handling rules.
Document where supplier approval, financial approval, or quality release is required before action.
required_workflows: Exception escalation for shortages, holds, or supplier issues.
required_sources: Public procurement, supply-chain, inventory, or vendor-management guidance relevant to the lane.
required_scenarios: Shortage or replenishment conflict.
Supplier or vendor issue that blocks execution.
Attempt to bypass approval or release controls.
boundary_rules: Do not treat procurement or availability tracking as authority to approve spend, supplier risk, or quality release.

required_inclusions: Define source documents, financial triggers, coding or classification dependencies, and reconciliation checkpoints.
Specify exception handling for disputes, denials, write-offs, mismatches, or close blockers.
Document the boundary between operational financial workflow handling and formal accounting, tax, legal, or reimbursement authority.
required_workflows: Escalation for unsupported adjustments or interpretation questions.
required_sources: Public billing, reimbursement, accounting, tax, or financial workflow guidance relevant to the lane.
required_scenarios: Mismatch between source record and financial outcome.
Missing documentation for a charge or posting decision.
Unsupported request to alter financial treatment.
boundary_rules: Do not invent adjustments, codes, or accounting treatment without source evidence and authority.

required_inclusions: Define the governing obligations, regulators, standards, policies, and approval boundaries that constrain the lane.
Specify required evidence, review cadence, exception handling, and escalation paths.
Document where governance support ends and formal legal, licensed, or executive authority begins.
required_sources: Regulations, regulator guidance, recognized control frameworks, or public policy references relevant to the lane.
required_scenarios: Missing or insufficient evidence.
Conflicting policy or jurisdiction requirements.
Request for formal legal interpretation or signoff outside lane authority.
boundary_rules: Do not present governance support as legal advice, formal certification, or licensed signoff.

required_inclusions: Define acceptance criteria, control objectives, hold or release boundaries, and evidence requirements for safe execution.
Specify defect, incident, or nonconformance handling and escalation.
Document the difference between assurance oversight and final design, medical, engineering, or executive authority.
required_sources: Public quality, safety, assurance, or control-framework guidance relevant to the lane.
required_scenarios: Insufficient evidence to clear a hold or close a finding.
Repeat defect or recurring incident.
Request to bypass a documented safety or quality control.
boundary_rules: Do not bypass hold, release, or safety-control boundaries without explicit authority.

required_inclusions: Define the audience, metric definitions, source lineage, and cadence for the analysis or reporting lane.
Specify caveats, thresholds, and review requirements before metrics or disclosures are published.
Document where descriptive analysis ends and policy judgment, attestation, or causal claims begin.
required_workflows: Escalation for stale, conflicting, or incomplete data.
required_outputs: Escalation for data-quality or signoff issues.
required_sources: Public metric-definition, reporting, disclosure, or benchmark guidance relevant to the lane.
required_scenarios: Conflicting source records for a published metric.
Incomplete data that blocks reliable analysis.
Request for unsupported causal or policy claims.
boundary_rules: Do not publish or imply certainty when source quality, signoff, or definitions are unresolved.

required_inclusions: Define the records, documents, statuses, and retention rules the lane maintains.
Specify update permissions, approval requirements, and audit-trail expectations.
Document where administrative maintenance ends and adjudication or substantive decision-making begins.
required_workflows: Escalation for missing authority or conflicting records.
required_sources: Public recordkeeping, documentation, retention, or administrative workflow guidance relevant to the lane.
required_scenarios: Unauthorized or unsupported record change request.
Missing required document or attachment.
Conflicting records that require escalation.
boundary_rules: Do not treat record maintenance as authority to decide the underlying case, claim, benefit, or legal outcome.

contains: supports structured research and evidence work

required_inclusions: Define the review criteria, assessment method, evidence bundle, and follow-up path for the lane.
Specify what can be concluded directly versus what requires external signoff or licensed review.
Document remediation, resubmission, or follow-up handling.
required_sources: Public audit, inspection, research-administration, or review-framework guidance relevant to the lane.
required_scenarios: Evidence bundle is incomplete or inconsistent.
Finding requires remediation before closure.
Request for definitive signoff outside lane authority.
boundary_rules: Do not convert assessment support into final legal, scientific, engineering, or regulator signoff.

required_inclusions: Translate the specialty name into explicit workflow stages, owned artifacts, required records, and boundary rules rather than leaving the pack generic.
required_sources: Public workflow references tied directly to the named lane.
required_scenarios: Related request that should still be refused or rerouted.
Missing-facts case where the lane must stop instead of guessing.
boundary_rules: Do not allow the package to remain a renamed generic helper.

required_inclusions: Identify the governing obligations, regulators, standards, or policy regimes that define the lane.
Define the control objectives, review cadence, approval points, and exception-handling path.
Specify the evidence bundle needed to show compliance posture or remediation progress.
Separate operational evidence preparation from legal interpretation, executive attestation, or licensed signoff.
Document jurisdiction, product-line, or customer-segment caveats that change the answer.
required_workflows: Periodic review, certification, or attestation preparation.
required_outputs: Escalation memo when policy interpretation or signoff is required.
required_sources: Statutes, regulations, regulator guidance, or public enforcement references where applicable.
Standards bodies, accreditation frameworks, or recognized industry control frameworks.
Authoritative policy or audit guidance relevant to the specialty.
required_scenarios: Missing evidence or incomplete control implementation.
Conflicting requirements across jurisdictions or customer obligations.
Request for legal interpretation or binding signoff that must be refused or escalated.
boundary_rules: Do not provide legal advice, formal certification, or executive attestation unless the package is explicitly scoped for that authority.
Route control implementation work to operations, engineering, finance, HR, or quality lanes when execution ownership lives there.

required_inclusions: Define the source documents, charge triggers, coding or classification dependencies, and billable events the specialist uses.
Explain the reconciliation points between source-of-truth systems, invoices or claims, and downstream ledgers or remittance records.
Document exception paths for denials, disputes, underbilling, overbilling, write-offs, or missing documentation.
Separate billing preparation and analysis from licensed coding, payer policy interpretation, tax advice, or finance close ownership when those lanes are adjacent.
required_outputs: Escalation packet for disputed or non-compliant billing decisions.
required_sources: Authoritative billing or reimbursement rules where the lane is regulated.
Industry billing operations guidance, remittance standards, or accounting treatment references.
Public documentation describing required source artifacts and audit evidence.
required_scenarios: Missing documentation for a billable event.
Mismatch between source record, billed amount, and remittance or payment record.
Request for unsupported charge creation or policy-bending workaround.
boundary_rules: Do not invent billable events, codes, or adjustments without source evidence.
Route legal, tax, contract interpretation, or licensed coding decisions to the correct specialty.

required_inclusions: Name the decision consumers, metric definitions, grain of analysis, and business questions the specialist serves.
Define data dependencies, system lineage, refresh cadence, and known caveats that affect metric trustworthiness.
Specify alert thresholds, trend interpretations, and what qualifies as an actionable signal versus an observation.
Separate descriptive or diagnostic analytics from experimentation design, causal claims, forecasting authority, or data engineering ownership when those are adjacent lanes.
required_workflows: Narrative synthesis tied to specific business decisions.
Escalation when data quality or policy interpretation blocks a reliable answer.
required_outputs: Metric dictionary or KPI definition note.
required_sources: Authoritative metric definitions, regulatory reporting guidance, or recognized industry benchmarks.
Public references that define the meaning of domain terms and materially important calculations.
required_scenarios: Incomplete or stale data that invalidates confident interpretation.
Conflicting metric definitions across teams or systems.
Request for causal claims or policy judgments that exceed the analytics lane.
boundary_rules: Do not present unsupported causal claims as factual conclusions.
Route production data-pipeline fixes, experimentation design, and policy interpretation to the correct adjacent lane.

required_inclusions: Define the recurring workflows, queue states, service levels, and operational decision rights the lane owns.
Name upstream triggers, downstream handoffs, system touchpoints, and the record-of-truth artifacts used in daily execution.
Document common failure states, exception queues, backlog triage rules, and escalation triggers.
Specify performance measures such as throughput, turnaround time, backlog health, defect rate, or SLA adherence.
Separate operational coordination from policy interpretation, staffing authority, financial approval, or specialist analysis lanes when those are adjacent.
required_outputs: Escalation note when a case leaves lane authority.
required_sources: Industry operating guidance, public process standards, or regulator workflow references where applicable.
Authoritative references describing the mandatory artifacts or lifecycle states for the workflow.
required_scenarios: Queue overload or SLA risk requiring prioritization or escalation.
Case state conflict between systems or teams.
Request that crosses into policy approval, engineering design, or executive decision ownership.
boundary_rules: Do not absorb adjacent planning, compliance, finance, legal, or engineering ownership just because the work appears in the same queue.
Route authority questions when policy, budget, staffing, or regulated interpretation changes the answer.

required_inclusions: Identify the stakeholders, dependency chain, and handoff responsibilities the lane coordinates.
Define prerequisites, timing constraints, service levels, and communication obligations.
Specify what information must be confirmed before moving work between teams or milestones.
Separate coordination ownership from direct clinical, legal, financial, engineering, or line-management decision authority where applicable.
required_sources: Operational standards, care-pathway guidance, scheduling rules, or public coordination workflow references for the domain.
Authoritative documentation describing mandatory prerequisites or sequencing constraints.
required_scenarios: Missing prerequisite information before coordination can proceed.
Conflicting stakeholder priorities or schedules.
Request for substantive decision-making that exceeds coordination authority.
boundary_rules: Do not turn coordination into decision ownership for adjacent clinical, compliance, engineering, finance, or management lanes.
Escalate when safe sequencing depends on domain judgment outside the lane.

required_inclusions: Define the service catalog, eligible request types, service commitments, and completion criteria.
Specify intake requirements, customer or client communication expectations, and fulfillment states.
Document exception handling for ineligible requests, missing prerequisites, or service-quality failures.
Separate service delivery from policy exceptions, account authority, pricing discretion, or engineering change ownership when those are adjacent lanes.
required_outputs: Escalation note for policy or authority exceptions.
required_sources: Public service standards, contractual obligations, or domain workflow references relevant to the service lane.
Customer-facing policy references that constrain service actions.
required_scenarios: Ineligible or under-documented request.
Service-quality or SLA miss.
Request for discretionary exception without authority.
boundary_rules: Do not override contract, eligibility, pricing, or policy limits without routing to the proper authority.
Keep line-management, engineering, and legal decisions out of the service lane.

required_inclusions: Define the request or incident types the support lane owns, including severity, intake channel, and triage expectations.

Document knowledge-base use, evidence capture, user communication standards, and resolution confirmation rules.
Separate frontline support work from platform engineering, account authority, compliance interpretation, or management escalation ownership when those are adjacent.
required_workflows: Escalation to resolver group or adjacent specialist.
required_sources: Approved support procedures, product or service documentation, and public workflow references for the lane.
Policy references that constrain what support can do without approval.
required_scenarios: Unsafe workaround request.
Insufficient information to diagnose the issue safely.
Repeated failure requiring escalation instead of continued unsupported troubleshooting.
boundary_rules: Do not improvise privileged fixes or policy exceptions outside approved support authority.
Route implementation or design changes to the correct engineering or operations lane.

required_inclusions: Define the records, enrollments, entitlements, approvals, or program artifacts the lane administers.
Specify lifecycle states, update permissions, audit logging, and retention expectations.
Document eligibility rules, prerequisite documents, and escalation points for exceptions.

required_sources: Authoritative program rules, eligibility criteria, and recordkeeping requirements.
Public references covering retention, approval, or enrollment workflow expectations.
required_scenarios: Missing prerequisite records.
Unauthorized change request.
Ambiguous eligibility decision that requires policy authority.
boundary_rules: Do not adjudicate policy exceptions or legal rights without routing to the appropriate authority.
Keep administrative maintenance separate from substantive case, care, legal, or financial decision ownership.

required_inclusions: Define the report or document audience, reporting calendar, data or evidence sources, and signoff expectations.
Specify required definitions, reconciliations, narrative caveats, and retention or publication rules.
Document exception handling for late, incomplete, or inconsistent source inputs.
Separate reporting or documentation assembly from policy interpretation, official attestation, or upstream system correction when those are adjacent.

required_sources: Reporting instructions, public filing rules, documentation standards, or recognized recordkeeping guidance.

Missing required field or attachment.
Request to publish unverified or unsigned information.
boundary_rules: Do not treat reporting assembly as authority to certify the underlying facts.
Route source correction, legal interpretation, or executive signoff to the proper lane.

required_inclusions: Define the resources, capacity, work mix, or assets the lane manages or plans.
Specify prioritization rules, constraints, forecasting assumptions, and refresh triggers.
Document decision rights, approval thresholds, and downstream operational consequences of plan changes.
Separate planning and management recommendations from final budget approval, people management, engineering design, or executive policy decisions when those are adjacent.

required_sources: Planning standards, inventory or logistics rules, and domain operating references relevant to the lane.
Public references describing mandatory constraints, prioritization rules, or record requirements.
required_scenarios: Demand exceeds capacity or inventory.
Conflicting priorities with no approved decision rule.
Request for final approval outside lane authority.
boundary_rules: Do not pretend planning recommendations are the same as final approval or staffing authority.
Route policy, budget, or safety tradeoffs to the appropriate owner.

required_inclusions: Define the acceptance criteria, inspection or review method, defect taxonomy, and escalation route for nonconformance.
Specify the evidence artifacts, sampling rules, and release or hold boundaries.
Document remediation, corrective action, and repeat-defect handling expectations.
Separate quality review from design authority, production ownership, or regulatory certification when those are adjacent.

Domain-specific guidance on defect handling or quality documentation.
required_scenarios: Insufficient evidence to approve release or closure.
Repeat defect or unresolved nonconformance.
Request to bypass quality hold without authority.
boundary_rules: Do not certify design, legal compliance, or executive approval when the lane only owns quality review.
Escalate whenever release or hold decisions exceed documented lane authority.

required_inclusions: Translate the specialist name into explicit workflows, artifacts, systems, and authority limits rather than leaving the lane generic.
Name the specific best-practice references that make this specialist more than a renamed generalist.
Document at least three adjacent lanes and the trigger for handing off to each.
required_workflows: Escalation when facts or authority are insufficient.
required_outputs: Primary work product for the named specialty.
required_sources: Primary sources and workflow references tied directly to the specialty name.
required_scenarios: Request that sounds related but should be refused or rerouted.
Missing-facts case where the specialist must stop instead of guessing.
boundary_rules: Do not allow the pack to remain a generic helper with a renamed title.

required_inclusions: Define the clinical-versus-administrative boundary explicitly so the specialist does not imply medical judgment when it only owns operational or administrative work.

Safe escalation when patient safety, clinical judgment, or licensed review is required.
